segments.caption: The video begins with a man with a beard and glasses, wearing a black t-shirt with the text 'freeCodeCamp(🔥)', standing in a vibrant, tech-themed room filled with shelves of gadgets, robots, and colorful lighting. He speaks directly to the camera, likely introducing a topic. The scene transitions to a solid blue screen with the white text 'What is a computer?'. This is followed by a series of animated illustrations: a woman in a blue sweater using an orange laptop, a smartwatch displaying time and heart rate, a person wearing a VR headset, and a desktop monitor with the text 'What exactly is a computer?' being typed. segments.caption: The video begins with a dark blue background filled with scrolling binary code, symbolizing digital data, as small animated icons for photo, video, and game appear sequentially. The computer’s screen shows a smiley face, and a keyboard and mouse appear. Next, a dynamic array of app icons—including Windows, Apple, Chrome, Instagram, Facebook, and others—falls into place, forming a horizontal row. The video then displays a desktop monitor with a Windows-style interface, followed by a laptop screen with a macOS-like interface, and finally a smartphone being held by a hand, showing a login screen with a profile icon. The phone screen transitions to display the Apple logo, suggesting a device switch or login process.

segments.caption: The video begins with a title card reading 'Buttons and Ports on a Computer' against a solid teal background. It then transitions to a split-screen view showing the back of a desktop monitor, a desktop tower, and a laptop, each with various ports highlighted. The camera pans across the devices, focusing on different ports such as USB, USB-C, audio jacks, and power connectors. The power button is highlighted on each device — the desktop tower, monitor, and laptop — with a yellow power icon enclosed in a pink box. The video then demonstrates the power cable being plugged into the monitor and laptop, emphasizing the circular power port. Next, the USB ports are highlighted with a pink box and labeled 'USB', followed by illustrations of common USB-connected devices like a keyboard, printer, and flash drives. The video then shifts focus to USB-C ports, similarly labeled and shown on both the monitor and laptop, with a close-up of the smaller, oval-shaped port. The overall visual style is clean and minimalist, using flat colors and simple shapes to clearly illustrate computer hardware components.

segments.caption: The video begins with a detailed illustration of a computer's internal components, focusing first on the RAM (Random Access Memory). A green RAM module is shown inserted into a motherboard, with text explaining that RAM is short-term memory, cannot store files, and is cleared when the computer is turned off. The scene transitions to highlight the hard drive, depicted as a mechanical disk drive with a read/write arm, labeled as long-term storage. This is followed by a comparison with a solid-state drive (SSD), shown as a circuit board with memory chips, noted as being faster, more durable, and more expensive. segments.caption: The video begins with a clean white screen displaying the text 'macOS' in a simple, modern font, centered with a subtle rainbow gradient line above it. The scene transitions to a stylized purple laptop with a teal screen showing a Windows logo, suggesting a cross-platform theme. Next, a white laptop appears with a dark purple screen, displaying a window with a grid of icons and a dock at the bottom, resembling a macOS interface. The scene shifts to a dark purple background where a white tablet and smartphone appear, both showing a lock screen with '12:00'. A hand interacts with the devices, unlocking them and revealing a calendar interface on the tablet and a grid of app icons on the phone. The focus then moves to a large app icon labeled 'Top Dog' featuring a pink dog with sunglasses and stars, with a checklist indicating compatibility with iOS and macOS, and question marks for Windows and Android. The app icon is shown across multiple devices—a desktop, tablet, phone, and laptop—emphasizing cross-platform availability. The scene transitions to a stylized animated woman with dark hair and a teal sweater, sitting at a desk with a laptop, books, and a plant, in a light blue room with a window. She wears a teal and yellow 'Top Dog' cap and is actively using the laptop. The video then displays a title card with 'Understanding Applications' in white text on a dark blue background with wavy lines. A red smartphone appears, showing '7:00', and a hand taps the screen to play a video. The video plays on a tablet held by two hands, showing a woman in a pink shirt. The word 'APPLICATIONS' appears in large white letters on the dark blue background, concluding the segment.

segments.caption: The video begins with a close-up of two hands holding a black cable with a white connector, set against a pale yellow background, introducing the theme of computer connections. The scene transitions to a diagram comparing modern and legacy video connectors: HDMI and USB-C are shown on the left in a yellow section, while VGA and DVI are displayed on the right in a teal section, each labeled with their respective names. The narrative then shifts to a desktop setup on a wooden desk with a green wall backdrop, featuring a monitor, keyboard, mouse, speakers, and a desktop tower. A hand plugs a black cable into the tower’s HDMI port, then into the monitor’s corresponding port, demonstrating the monitor connection process. The scene changes to a pink background with the text 'Keyboard & Mouse' in a rectangular frame, followed by illustrations of a keyboard and mouse connected via USB cables. The hand then plugs a USB cable into the tower’s USB port, and another into the keyboard’s port, completing the peripheral setup. Finally, a top-down view shows the fully connected desktop system with animated lines illustrating the data flow between the tower, monitor, keyboard, and mouse, culminating in a yellow background with a wireless keyboard and tower, indicating wireless connectivity options.

segments.caption: The video begins with a yellow background showing a wireless keyboard and a desktop tower connected via a Wi-Fi signal, introducing the theme of computer setup. The scene transitions to a document titled 'Instructions' with bullet points, suggesting a step-by-step guide. Next, a light blue background displays the text 'Speakers/Headphones', followed by a hand plugging a green audio cable into the back of a desktop tower, with the setup shown on a wooden desk alongside a monitor and speakers. The video then highlights the 'However' text, indicating a potential complication, before showing various audio peripherals—speakers, headphones, and a microphone—each with USB connectors. A pink background appears with the text 'Connect the Power', leading to a close-up of a hand plugging a power cable into the desktop tower, then into a power strip, and finally into a wall outlet. The sequence concludes with a bald man peeking over the monitor with a satisfied expression, indicating successful setup, all set against a green background with the desktop components neatly arranged on a wooden desk.

segments.caption: The video begins with a bald cartoon man peeking from behind a wooden desk, smiling as he looks at a desktop computer setup consisting of a black monitor, keyboard, mouse, two white speakers, and a silver tower. He presses the power button on the tower, and the monitor turns on, displaying a colorful game screen with a pink dog wearing sunglasses and the text 'Top Dog' and 'Start New Game'. The scene transitions to a blue title card reading 'Connecting to the Internet' over a house illustration. The view then zooms into the house, showing a laptop on a table inside, displaying a web browser with a search bar and a list of results. The user clicks on a result titled 'Basketball Dog', which opens to a page showing a dog playing basketball. The scene shifts to a floor plan of the house, revealing a router emitting pink Wi-Fi signals, connecting the laptop to the internet. Additional devices—a tablet and a smartphone—appear in other rooms, also connected to the Wi-Fi. The video then transitions to an outdoor view, showing various internet connection cables (dial-up, DSL, cable) entering the house. Text labels appear: 'Dial-up' with a speedometer indicating 'Slower', followed by 'DSL' and 'Cable' with arrows pointing to 'phone line' and 'cable TV connection' respectively, illustrating the different types of internet connections.

segments.caption: The video begins with a comparison of internet connection types, starting with DSL and Cable, each represented by their respective cables (orange for DSL, black for Cable) and labeled as 'Broadband'. A speedometer graphic appears below, indicating 'Faster' as the needle moves toward the red zone, followed by a laptop displaying a penguin on a green screen, symbolizing Linux. The scene transitions to Fiber, shown with a yellow cable splitting into two blue connectors, labeled 'Fiber', with a speedometer now indicating 'Fastest' and the needle at maximum speed. Text appears stating 'May not be available' and '$$$', highlighting cost and availability issues. The next segment introduces Cellular internet, with a gray cell tower emitting pink waves and a smartphone displaying '4G' and then '5G' on its screen, showing time as '12:45'. The phone is labeled as a 'Home option' and 'Good broadband alternative'. The final scene displays all four connection types—Cellular tower, Fiber, Cable, and DSL—under a pink bracket labeled 'Internet Service Provider' (ISP), summarizing the different technologies offered by ISPs.

segments.caption: The video begins with a diagram illustrating the role of an Internet Service Provider (ISP), showing connections to a phone company and a cable company, with a pink arrow labeled 'Shop around' pointing downward, suggesting consumer choice. The scene transitions to an ISP van driving along a road past suburban houses, symbolizing service delivery. Inside a home, a hand places a modem on a table, and the modem is shown connected to a wall outlet via a black cable, with green indicator lights turning on. A yellow Ethernet cable connects the modem to a desktop computer, which then displays a browser with a search bar. The setup expands to include a Wi-Fi network, with a router appearing and emitting pink wireless signals, connecting a smartphone, tablet, and laptop to the network. The text 'Wi-Fi Network' appears in pink at the bottom, emphasizing the wireless connectivity. The final scene shows all devices actively using the internet, with the laptop displaying a webpage of blue shoes, indicating successful network access.

segments.caption: The video begins with a wide view of a home Wi-Fi network setup, showing a modem connected to a router via a yellow Ethernet cable, with the router emitting pink Wi-Fi signals. Various devices—a smartphone, desktop computer, monitor, and laptop—are shown connected to the network, with the laptop displaying a webpage of blue shoes. The scene transitions to a close-up of the modem and router, highlighting the connection between them. A 'Modem + Router' box appears, illustrating the combined device. The focus then shifts to the router’s wireless setup interface, where the network name 'Jim Wireless' is entered, WPA2 security is selected, and a password is set, represented by black dots. The video then shows a smartphone’s Wi-Fi menu, where 'Jim Wireless' is selected from a list of available networks. The scene returns to the physical setup, showing the modem, router, desktop, and monitor connected via cables. A 'Wireless Card' box appears, indicating the internal component enabling wireless connectivity. The video concludes with a house layout, showing the router in the center emitting Wi-Fi signals to multiple rooms, where a smartphone, tablet, and laptop are connected and actively using the network, with the tablet displaying a list and the laptop showing a 'Basketball Dog' webpage.

segments.caption: The video begins with a two-story house illustration showing multiple devices—smartphones, a tablet, and a laptop—connected wirelessly to a central router, each displaying different content like lists, social media, games, and shopping, with pink Wi-Fi signals radiating from the router. The scene transitions to a blue screen with the text 'What is the Cloud' in white. Next, a stylized man with a red headband, teal shirt, and blue pants sits at a yellow desk using a laptop in a room with a window and a clock on the wall. Suddenly, a meteor strikes the window, shattering it and causing debris, and the man looks shocked. The scene shifts to a thought bubble above his head, showing a white cloud filled with icons representing music, photos, and documents, symbolizing cloud storage. The cloud then connects to two server racks via colorful cables, illustrating data storage. A laptop appears in front of the servers, displaying a 'SEARCH' interface, with bubbles floating around. The video then shows a grid of six app icons—document, photo, music, cloud, file, and spreadsheet—before zooming into a web browser labeled 'MY PHOTOS' with a grid of landscape images. The text 'WEB APPS' appears over the browser. The scene changes to a smartphone screen with the same six app icons, and a yellow hand taps the cloud icon, opening a Google Docs interface with documents titled 'Recipes' and 'Pizza Sales'.

segments.caption: The video begins with a close-up of a smartphone screen displaying a document titled 'Pizza Sale' with a pie chart, as a yellow hand taps the screen to interact with the content. The scene transitions to show the same document displayed across multiple devices—a smartphone, a tablet, and a laptop—emphasizing cross-device accessibility. The setting then shifts to a colorful street where a stylized man with a red headband walks past storefronts, one of which has a toaster in the window. The focus returns to a hand holding a smartphone, now showing a photo of a white rabbit holding a pizza slice, which is then uploaded to a cloud service. The rabbit appears on a TV screen, followed by a woman on a tropical beach, symbolizing remote access. A cloud icon filled with music, photos, and documents appears, representing cloud storage, which is then shown being secured against a red skull-and-crossbones icon, indicating data protection. The cloud is labeled 'CLOUD-BASED STORAGE' with backup folders listed, and data flows from the cloud to multiple server racks, illustrating distributed storage. segments.caption: The video begins with an animated sequence illustrating cloud backup functionality. segments.caption: The video begins with a close-up of the back of a desktop computer tower, showing various cables plugged into its ports, including a white USB cable, a blue VGA cable, and a black power cable. The scene transitions to a woman with short brown hair, wearing a gray button-up shirt over a red-and-white striped top, holding a black HP keyboard upside down, inspecting its underside. She then places the keyboard on a white desk and uses a can of compressed air to blow out dust from between the keys. Next, she sprays a cleaning solution from a white bottle onto a white cloth and wipes the keyboard’s surface and keys thoroughly. The setting shifts to a kitchen sink, where she rinses the keyboard under running water, tilting it to allow water to flow through the keys. After rinsing, she turns off the faucet and places the keyboard on the counter. The video then cuts to a close-up of a black wired mouse being held upside down, revealing its optical sensor and feet, with the red light of the sensor illuminated. The woman’s hands are visible throughout, and the environment transitions from an office desk to a kitchen sink, indicating a thorough cleaning process for computer peripherals.

segments.caption: The video begins with two laptops side-by-side on an orange background: one displays a pink skull-and-crossbones symbol, while the other shows multiple windows labeled with sensitive data like 'SSN', 'Bank acct', 'credit card', and 'email acct', illustrating malware threats. Text appears below listing types of malware: 'VIRUSES', 'SPYWARE', 'TROJAN HORSES', 'RANSOMWARE', and 'KEYLOGGERS'. The scene transitions to three devices—a laptop, desktop monitor, and another laptop—showing clean interfaces, then all three simultaneously display the pink skull-and-crossbones, indicating infection. The background shifts to green, and a stylized animated person with spiky hair appears behind a laptop, which now shows an 'Antivirus' interface with icons for lock, shield, laptop, and globe. Text lists antivirus software: 'Kaspersky', 'Norton', 'Windows Defender'. The antivirus scan progresses, showing 'scanning..' and 'VIRUSES REMOVED: 2', then 'complete!' and 'VIRUSES REMOVED: 3'. A pop-up appears with 'YOU WIN!!!' and a 'COLLECT MONEY' button. The screen then shows an 'Antivirus update available' notification with 'remind me later' and 'update now' buttons. The user clicks 'update now', and the screen displays 'updating...'. The scene transitions to a colorful wavy background with text boxes for 'Storage Sense' and 'Defragment & Optimize Drives'. Finally, a blue desktop interface appears with a 'Settings' icon highlighted by a yellow arrow, indicating the next step in system maintenance.

segments.caption: The video begins with a blue desktop interface showing a taskbar with a search bar and a settings icon, both highlighted by yellow arrows. The user clicks the settings icon, opening the 'Configure Storage Sense or run it now' window. The toggle for Storage Sense is switched from 'Off' to 'On', and the frequency is changed from 'Run every day' to 'Run every week' via a dropdown menu. The 'Clean now' button is clicked, initiating a cleanup process that completes with a message indicating 72.4MB of disk space was freed. The scene transitions to the 'Disk Cleanup' window, showing a list of files to delete with checkboxes, followed by the 'Optimize Drives' window where the 'Optimize' button is clicked, starting a drive optimization process that reaches 40% before the 'Change settings' button is clicked. This opens the 'Optimization schedule' dialog, where the frequency is changed from 'Weekly' to 'Monthly'. The video then shifts to an animated scene of a person with spiky hair using a laptop, which suddenly emits smoke and fire, symbolizing a system crash. The text 'Back up your computer' appears, followed by an animation of a hand plugging an external hard drive into a laptop, with a progress bar on the screen indicating 'Transferring files...'. The final frame shows the text 'Windows Backup' over a gray background.

The narrative shifts to a web browser interface where a search for 'online backup service' yields results like 'iDrive' and 'Carbonite,' leading to a webpage titled 'Online Cloud Backup' with pricing tiers for 'Basic' and 'Business' plans. A stylized cloud with multicolored outlines appears, with the word 'SAFE' emerging inside, emphasizing cloud security. The cloud then connects to a laptop via colorful data streams, illustrating data transfer. The scene changes to a green background with a simple line-drawn person with spiky hair using a laptop, followed by a title card 'Creating a Safe Workspace.' The final scene shows a man in a red shirt and blue pants sitting at a blue desk with a monitor and keyboard, appearing strained, which transitions to a banner held by hands reading 'Ergonomics!'—highlighting the importance of physical comfort in computing environments. The background shifts from beige to green to blue, reflecting the evolving focus from digital safety to physical workspace safety.

segments.caption: The video begins with a stylized desktop setup featuring a monitor, keyboard, and a hand using a red mouse. On the monitor, a sun icon and a horizontal slider are shown, with a cursor adjusting the slider from left to right, changing the screen’s color temperature from cooler to warmer. The screen then displays a 'Night Shift' interface with labels 'Less Warm' and 'More Warm', and the cursor moves the slider to the 'More Warm' end, indicating a preference for warmer screen tones. segments.caption: The video segment begins with a close-up of a Gmail interface displaying a spam email purportedly from 'Service@WellsFargo.com', which is actually a phishing attempt. The email, marked as spam by Gmail, contains a deceptive message claiming the recipient's Wells Fargo account has been locked after three failed login attempts and includes a suspicious link to 'http://www.wellsfargo.com/webscr/login_submit'. A yellow warning banner explains why the message was flagged as spam. The word 'Phishing' appears in large white text over the email to emphasize the threat. The user then clicks on the link, which opens a new browser tab. The address bar shows the URL being typed, and the user attempts to navigate to the fake login page. The scene transitions to another phishing email, this time from 'bankofamerica@securesuite.net', falsely claiming the recipient's Bank of America account is restricted and requiring reactivation via a provided link. The email includes a red banner with the Bank of America logo and a 'Security Update Notification' heading, designed to mimic official communication. The overall environment is a standard web browser interface with visible tabs, address bar, and Gmail navigation elements, highlighting the dangers of phishing emails and the importance of verifying URLs and sender addresses.

segments.caption: The video begins with a close-up of a macOS Maps application displaying a detailed map of Raleigh, North Carolina, with major roads, parks, and landmarks like Lake Johnson Park and Raleigh-Durham International Airport clearly labeled. The user then performs a keyboard shortcut, Control+Command+F, which causes the Maps window to enter full-screen mode, expanding to fill the entire display. Next, the user clicks the Apple menu in the top-left corner of the screen, revealing a dropdown menu with options such as 'About This Mac', 'System Preferences...', 'Dock', 'Recent Items', 'Sleep', 'Restart...', 'Shut Down...', and 'Log Out Lisa Roscoe...'. This pane shows options for 'Scroll direction: natural', 'Secondary click', and 'Smart zoom', along with a tracking slider and a visual representation of a hand using a white mouse. The mouse battery level is displayed as 45% at the bottom of the window. The user interacts with the 'Secondary click' dropdown, which is set to 'Click on right side', and the visual demonstration on the right side of the pane updates to show a finger clicking the right side of the mouse. The user then moves the tracking slider slightly to the right, increasing the mouse speed, and the visual demonstration continues to show the hand moving the mouse. Overlaid on this image is a weather widget for 'Raleigh, NC' showing 89°F, 'Partly Cloudy', and a low of 71°F, with a 'Full Forecast' link. A prominent headline reads, 'The Damage is Really Unprecedented and Very Widespread,' with a blue 'Read the story' button below it. On the right, a section titled 'Our Favorite Things' features a video thumbnail with the text 'This Guy is Facing Off Against a Barracuda, It’s Not the First Time Either.' The browser's address bar shows the URL 'www.weather.com,' and the tab title is 'National and Local Weather.' The overall environment is a digital interface with a clear, bright, and informative layout, focused on weather and news content.

entity_library.Maps: A macOS Maps application displaying a map of Raleigh, North Carolina, with a blue dot indicating the user's location, major roads, and landmarks like Lake Johnson Park and Raleigh-Durham International Airport. The map is initially in windowed mode, then enters full-screen mode via a keyboard shortcut, and returns to windowed mode.
entity_library.System Preferences: A macOS System Preferences window with a grid of icons for various settings, including 'General', 'Displays', 'Energy Saver', 'Keyboard', 'Mouse', 'Trackpad', 'Sound', 'iCloud', 'Internet Accounts', 'Network', 'Bluetooth', 'Sharing', 'Users & Groups', 'Parental Controls', 'App Store', 'Dictation & Speech', 'Date & Time', 'Startup Disk', 'Time Machine', and 'Accessibility'. The user clicks on the 'Mouse' icon.
entity_library.Mouse preferences: The Mouse preferences pane within System Preferences, showing options for 'Scroll direction: natural', 'Secondary click' (set to 'Click on right side'), 'Smart zoom', and a tracking slider. A visual demonstration on the right side of the window shows a hand using a white mouse, and the mouse battery level is displayed as 45% at the bottom.
entity_library.Desktop background: A blue desktop background with a repeating pattern of concentric circles, visible when the Maps application is in full-screen mode and around the edges of the System Preferences window.
entity_library.Dock: The macOS Dock at the bottom of the screen, containing application icons including Finder, Safari, Mail, Calendar, Chrome, Firefox, and others, with the Maps application icon highlighted when active.
entity_library.Etsy homepage: The official Etsy website with a banner reading 'Shop directly from people around the world', featuring a photo of a person working with wood and a highlighted shop named 'DOCKSMITH' from Brunswick, Maine.
entity_library.DOCKSMITH shop page: The Etsy shop page for 'DOCKSMITH', displaying the shop's name, description 'Driftwood Docking Stations For Smartphones and Tablets. Made in Maine...', a navigation menu on the left with categories like 'PHONES', 'SMALL TABLETS', and 'LARGE TABLETS', and product images on the right.
entity_library.Back button: The browser's back navigation button, used to return from the DOCKSMITH shop page to the main Etsy homepage.
entity_library.Browser navigation elements: The browser's address bar, back button, and tab interface, including the 'New Tab' button and the star icon for bookmarking, used to navigate between Etsy and weather.com.
entity_library.Weather.com homepage: The homepage of The Weather Channel, showing a large image of a man and child by a lake, with a weather widget indicating 89°F and partly cloudy conditions for Raleigh, NC, and a headline reading 'The Damage is Really Unprecedented and Very Widespread'.
